radiograph-defects.brief:
600x340 holographic navy/cyan simulated weld radiograph: a horizontal film strip (rounded corners, faint corner ID tags) viewed as a negative, with a weld seam band running left-to-right through center reading slightly LIGHTER than the darker parent-metal fields on either side (more metal in beam path = lighter). Animatable reveal ids, timed against the beat-3 narration 'On film, trapped slag and unfused sidewalls image dark. Tungsten is denser than steel, so its fragments print as sharp light spots.' (~8.8s total, on-screen note appears at 7.9s): rdfFilm = base film strip + weld band + edge tags, visible from 0s. rdfSlag (reveal ~1.2s, on 'trapped slag') = two parallel, irregular, broken DARK lines riding the two groove-face lines just inside the weld band edges (classic wagon-tracks geometry), small mono caption 'TRAPPED SLAG - DARK'. rdfLof (reveal ~2.6s, on 'unfused sidewalls') = one long, smooth, perfectly straight DARK line hugging ONE fusion face of the weld band, caption 'LACK OF FUSION - DARK'. rdfTungsten (reveal ~4.6s, on 'Tungsten is denser') = a single small, sharp BRIGHT white speck with subtle cyan glow near the root/centerline, caption 'TUNGSTEN - LIGHT'; it must be the only bright element so it pops against everything else. Keep the two dark indications clearly darker than the weld band. Do NOT draw porosity or any other indication types (narration does not cover them).
---
600x340: four simulated radiograph film windows in a 2x2 grid (each ~270x140) rendered as film density — lighter part background with soft-edged DARK indications (not engineering linework) — each with a small caption label. Diagram beat narration (23 w, ~8.3 s): 'On film: gas porosity, smooth round dark spots; shrinkage, feathery and branchy; cold shuts, smooth lines, rounded ends; hot tears, ragged at fillets.' Reveals in narration order, each = window frame lights + indication draws + label fades: rgdGas at 0.8 (window 1 'GAS POROSITY': scattered smooth round/elliptical dark spots with a near-surface pinhole cluster); rgdShrink at 3.0 (window 2 'SHRINKAGE': feathery dendritic branching dark network spreading from a heavy-section zone); rgdColdShut at 4.5 (window 3 'COLD SHUT': one smooth continuous dark line with clearly ROUNDED ends); rgdHotTear at 6.6 (window 4 'HOT TEAR': ragged, branched dark line tracing a drawn fillet at a section change). On-screen note fires at 7.5. Scope this asset to casting signatures only (no weld defects) despite the generic canonical name.

bar-roll-pass.brief: Title-beat panel (loopable, ambient), 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Longitudinal cutaway of round bar mid-reduction: stocky billet enters left into a grooved two-high roll pass (counter-rotating rolls biting a ROUND profile — explicitly not the flat plate of rolling-mill), slimmer round bar exits right with a visible diameter step at the bite. Inside the entering billet: small V-shaped pipe-cavity remnant, cluster of inclusion specks, dotted core segregation. The exiting bar carries those same features stretched into thin lines hugging the centerline: a fine cavity thread, wire-like stringers, a dotted segregated core. Narration over this beat (~9.5 s): 'Bar and rod are the ingot squeezed into one dimension — almost everything important inside ends up long, thin, and parallel to the axis.' Reveals: brpBite (t=1.5, on 'squeezed into one dimension') — roll rotation arrows plus a flow arrow through the bite; brpLines (t=4.5, on 'long, thin') — the three axial flaw lines draw out along the exiting bar; brpAxis (t=7.0, on 'parallel to the axis') — dashed centerline through the bar with mono uppercase tag PARALLEL TO THE AXIS. Labels mono uppercase cyan; keep base composition calm so it loops as a title panel.

bar-radial-scan.brief: Diagram-beat teaching asset, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Left ~55%: end-on cross-section of a 100 mm round steel bar (large circle, diameter dimension 'O 100 mm'), straight-beam contact probe seated at top dead center of the circumference with a thin couplant line, small rotation arrow on the rim (bar rotates under the probe), faint vertical diameter axis, and a small bright end-on reflector dot at the exact center (a stringer/pipe thread seen end-on, its length pointing out of the page). Right ~45%: mini A-scan strip — baseline with initial pulse at 0 and depth ticks 0 / 50 / 100 mm. Static base shows bar, probe, rotation arrow, center dot, empty A-scan. Narration over this beat (~9.5 s): 'Long reflectors sit broadside to a radial beam. On a 100 millimeter bar, the centerline echo lands near seventeen microseconds, the far wall near thirty-four.' Reveals: brsFlaw (t=1.2, on 'long reflectors sit broadside') — glow ring + ping on the centerline reflector with mono tag BROADSIDE; brsBeam (t=2.8, on 'radial beam') — cyan beam wedge firing from the probe face through the center to the far wall; brsEcho (t=5.5, on 'centerline echo lands near seventeen microseconds') — tall echo spike rises at the 50 mm tick with callout '50 mm · ~17 us'; brsBackwall (t=7.5, on 'far wall near thirty-four') — far-wall echo spike at the 100 mm tick drawn clearly SHORTER/dimmed with callout '~34 us' and small tag BACK WALL DROPS (the centerline reflector shadows it; velocity basis 5.92 mm/us longitudinal, round-trip times 16.9 and 33.8 us). All reveals complete before the spec note lands at 8.4 s. Text mono uppercase, numbers cyan.

mt-yoke.brief:
600x340 holographic navy/cyan. AC electromagnetic yoke: U-shaped laminated core with coil bulge in the handle, two articulated legs planted on a steel plate, hairline transverse crack midway between the poles. Animatable reveal ids: ykBody (yoke + steel part, the base scene), ykFlux (cyan flux lines arcing pole-to-pole THROUGH the part between the legs — a local longitudinal field; should support a gentle continuous loop), ykCrack (the hairline crack with a small leakage-flux plume escaping the surface), ykIndication (bright particle line snapping onto the crack). Timing vs MY narration: this lesson uses the asset as the beat-1 TITLE PANEL, so all four reveals are composed in their end state from 0s with ykFlux looping ambiently for the full ~9s beat. CONVERGENCE NOTE: mt1-1-2's spec already references this same canonical asset as a sequenced diagram beat (reveals fire in order: body/flux -> particles -> indication while its narration describes the continuous method); design once with these reveal ids so both lessons are served. No SVG exists in media-src/assets yet.
---
600x340 holographic navy/cyan title-panel hero: portable AC yoke straddling a steel plate butt weld, three-quarter view, two legs contacting the plate either side of the weld bead. Reveals: ykBase (draw, 0.0s) — plate, weld bead, and yoke body/legs draw on as the title card lands. ykFlux (fade, ~2.0s — narration 'earns its place by what it does well') — dashed flux arc flowing pole-to-pole through the plate beneath the weld, slow pulse loop. ykInd (ping+fade, ~5.0s — narration 'know exactly what it cannot do') — bright cyan particle indication blooming as a tight jagged line across the weld toe between the poles, small mono tag 'INDICATION', soft glow. Ambient loopable after ~6s (title beat runs ~8s). Canonical MT asset — will be reused as the course's signature title visual.
---
Converged MT hero asset, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan: AC electromagnetic yoke straddling a steel plate, flux lines arcing pole-to-pole through the part, a transverse surface crack between the legs, and dry particles dusting on then snapping into a crisp indication line over the crack. Reveals: ykFlux (draw) flux lines sweep between the poles; ykDust (fade) particle cloud drifts onto the surface; ykIndication (ping) particles snap into the indication line. Timing: drive discrete reveals from mt1-1-2's diagram beat ('Magnetize the part, then apply particles while the current is still flowing', ~9s: ykFlux ~1.5s, ykDust ~4.5s, ykIndication ~7.0s). For mt1-5-1 (and the other specs using it as a title panel: mt1-1-3, mt1-4-3, mt1-5-3) it renders as an ambient looping panel — labeled 'AC YOKE // INDICATION' here — so it needs a gentle idle state with flux shimmer and a glowing held indication.
---
MT-series hero panel (think pipe-scan for VT): an AC yoke straddling a flat steel plate, viewed in slight 3/4 perspective. Static base: yoke body with handle, two poles contacting the plate, plate surface with subtle machining speckle, and a tight transverse surface crack midway between the poles (crack runs perpendicular to the pole-to-pole axis). Bottom strip clear for the panel label chip (LIVE INDICATION style). Reveals timed to the 21-word title narration (~7.5s): ykFlux (draw, at 4.0) — flux loop lines flowing from one pole down through the plate to the other pole, visibly crowding INSIDE the steel, synced to 'Force it through cracked steel'; ykIndication (fade, at 5.9) + ykIndPing (ping, at 6.1) — a bright particle indication blooming along the crack where the flux crosses it, synced to 'the field betrays the flaw'. No text labels, no N/S markers — the indication and flux loop carry the story. Asset doubles as the recurring MT title panel across the course, so keep it iconic and uncluttered.
---
SHARED ASSET — also the diagram beat of mt1-1-2 (continuous method: particles applied while current flows) and the title panel of mt1-1-3; no SVG exists yet, so merge with any prior brief into ONE baked ~10 s timeline reading: energize, flux, particles, indication. 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. An AC yoke (U-shaped core with coil winding and handle) standing legs-down on a flat steel plate; a fine surface crack between the legs, roughly perpendicular to the leg-to-leg axis. Reveals: ykYoke (fade 0.5) yoke + plate base; ykFlux (draw 1.5) flux lines arc leg-to-leg through the plate with animated flow and an energized cue on the coil; ykParticles (fade 4.0) dry-powder specks dust across the plate between the legs; ykIndication (fade 6.5) particles gather into one bright crisp line on the crack; ykPing (fade 8.5) soft ping ring on the indication. In mt1-2-2 it plays as the ambient title-beat panel (label 'LIVE MT // INDICATION') under the hook 'Why does steel grab magnetic particles while aluminum ignores them?' — the dusting-then-gathering action must read clearly at panel scale; keep it loop-tolerant after ~8.5 s.
---
Canonical MT hero visual (600x340, holographic navy/cyan): steel plate with a fine transverse surface crack at center; C-shaped AC yoke straddling the crack, both poles in firm contact. Used as the TITLE PANEL for mt1-3-1 beat 1 (narration ~7s: 'Magnetic particle testing looks simple: magnetize, apply particles, read the indications. But a dirty surface quietly kills the test, without telling you.'). Required animatable reveal ids: ykFlux = flux lines energize and arc pole-to-pole through the plate between the yoke legs, fires on 'magnetize' (~1.5s); ykDust = fine particle dust drifts onto the surface between the poles, fires on 'apply particles' (~2.5s); ykIndication = particles snap into a crisp bright line along the crack with a small INDICATION tag, fires on 'read the indications' (~3.5-4s). After ykIndication, ambient flux shimmer loops for the remainder of the beat. Keep the part surface visibly bare/clean — this asset will be reused as the generic yoke visual across the MT course.
---
CANONICAL SHARED ASSET (also used as the title panel b

current-waveforms.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. 'Current Waveforms Used in MT' — three stacked rows, each pairing a waveform trace (left ~55%) with a steel cross-section depth panel (right ~45%) whose cyan shading shows how deep the magnetic field lives. Row 1 AC: sine wave crossing a zero axis; depth panel shows only a thin bright band hugging the top surface, tagged SKIN EFFECT. Row 2 HWDC: clipped sine — positive humps separated by flat dead gaps on the zero line (pulsating one-directional current); depth panel shading reaches moderate depth. Row 3 FWDC: continuous rectified ripple, back-to-back positive humps reading near-steady; deepest shading of the three. Shared right-edge depth arrow running surface-to-deep down the three panels. Animatable reveal ids vs MY beat-3 narration (~9.5s beat): cwAc (row 1 trace + skin-band panel + tag) in at ~0.4s during 'Alternating current reverses constantly, pinning its field to the surface — the skin effect'; cwHw (row 2) in at ~5.4s on 'Half-wave DC pulses and penetrates'; cwFw (row 3) in at ~7.2s on 'Full-wave DC drives the field deepest'; cwDepthArrow in at ~8.0s, just before the on-screen note lands at 8.5s.

xray-tube.brief:
Cutaway industrial directional X-ray tube, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Static base: sealed evacuated envelope outline; cathode assembly at left with a coiled filament seated in a focusing cup; copper anode block at right carrying a tungsten target face angled ~20 degrees to the electron path; beam window port at lower right. Minimal labels: CATHODE / FILAMENT, ANODE (COPPER), TUNGSTEN TARGET, WINDOW. Animatable reveals, in this order: (1) xtGlow — filament glows hot and a faint electron cloud shimmers around it; fires ~0.5-2.5s as my narration says 'A heated filament boils off electrons.' (2) xtStream — electron stream streaks left-to-right across the vacuum gap into the target, with cathode(-)/anode(+) high-voltage markers; ~3-5.5s on 'High voltage slams them into the tungsten target.' (3) xtFan — X-ray fan radiates from the angled focal spot out through the window with a small '~1%' tag; ~6-7.5s on 'About one percent becomes X rays.' (4) xtHeat — heat bloom spreads over the target/copper anode with a '~99% HEAT' tag; ~7.8-9.5s on 'the rest is heat,' coinciding with the beat note at 7.8s. CONVERGENCE NOTE: rt1-2-4 beat 2 uses this same asset with a near-identical narration arc (filament boils off electrons -> kilovoltage slams into tungsten -> ~1% X-rays -> rest is heat, noteAt 8.2); the same reveal order serves both lessons — design once.
---
X-ray tube cutaway, side view, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Horizontal evacuated glass envelope centered. Left: cathode with coiled tungsten filament. Right: tungsten target face angled ~20 deg, set in a copper anode block. Bottom-center: beam window with an X-ray fan exiting downward. Labels: FILAMENT (MA), KV across the vacuum gap, TUNGSTEN TARGET, COPPER, X-RAYS on the fan, HEAT at the anode. Beat runs ~9.5 s; narration: 'The filament boils off electrons; kilovoltage slams them into tungsten. Braking electrons radiate X rays — about one percent of the energy. The rest is heat.' Reveals: xtbFilament at 0.6 (filament coil glows white-hot — 'the filament'); xtbBoil at 1.2 (electron-cloud particles boiling off the cathode — 'boils off electrons'); xtbKv at 2.8 (KV label + acceleration arrows across the gap — 'kilovoltage'); xtbStream at 3.4 (electron stream draws left-to-right and strikes the angled target — 'slams them into tungsten'); xtbBeam at 5.2 (X-ray fan draws out the window — 'braking electrons radiate X rays'); xtbHeat at 7.0 (anode/copper heat glow + shimmer — 'the rest is heat'). Keep lower-right clear for the renderer note chip at 8.2 s ('BREMSSTRAHLUNG · ~1% X-RAYS · 99% HEAT').

xray-console.brief: Industrial X-ray control console hero panel, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan; title-beat panel, so composition should read at a glance and settle into a calm idle loop. Static base: console face with a kV dial, an mA dial, an exposure timer, a key switch, and an X-RAY ON indicator lamp; a high-voltage cable runs from the console to a small directional tube head at the right edge aimed at a flat test plate. Animatable reveals, in this order: (1) xcBeamOn — key switch flips, the X-RAY ON lamp illuminates, and a beam cone projects from the tube head onto the plate; fires ~1.5-3.5s as my narration says 'makes radiation only while energized.' (2) xcBeamOff — the lamp and beam cone extinguish and the console dims to idle standby; ~4.5-6.5s on 'Power off, hazard off.' After both reveals the scene holds a quiet idle glow while narration finishes 'Let's build the machine behind the beam.' Panel label shown by the player: CONTROL CONSOLE // BEAM ON.

laminate-stack.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Title-beat ambient panel: cross-section of a laminated composite being built. Base art: a build platform line; finished stack reads as 8-10 thin plies with alternating fiber-angle hatching (0/45/90 hatch directions) so it reads 'composite plies', not metal. A small normal-incidence probe sits on the top surface. One thin, flat lens-shaped gap lies BETWEEN two plies mid-stack, parallel to the surface, directly under the probe. Reveals: (1) lstPlies — plies slide/fade in one at a time bottom-up, syncs with narration 'a laminated composite is a stack of plies' (~0-3.5s); (2) lstFlaw — the thin flat inter-ply gap glows cyan, syncs with 'nearly every defect lies flat between those plies' (~4.5-7s); (3) lstBeam — probe pulses and a vertical beam line draws straight down onto the flat flaw face, syncs with 'perfectly oriented for a straight beam' (~7.5-9.5s). Used as a title-card panel, so it must idle/loop gracefully after all reveals fire.

delam-pulse-echo.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan, two-zone layout. LEFT: composite laminate cross-section with visible ply lines (distinct from the steel-plate 'lamination-ascan' asset — must read as plies, e.g. subtle fiber-angle hatching), probe on top surface, vertical beam path; a lens-shaped delamination (resin-to-air gap) between two plies at roughly mid-thickness. RIGHT: A-scan screen with baseline, front-surface entry signal at left of trace, and a healthy back-wall echo at right (present in base art). Reveals: (1) dpeDelam — the delamination gap highlights/opens between plies with an 'AIR' micro-label, syncs with 'a delamination is a resin-to-air interface' (~0-3.5s); (2) dpeEcho — a strong echo spike rises on the A-scan at the delamination's time position, ahead of the back-wall position, syncs with 'a strong echo at its depth' (~3.5-5.8s); (3) dpeDeadwall — the back-wall echo collapses/greys out with a small X or 'LOST' tag, syncs with 'the back wall goes dark' (~5.8-7.5s); (4) dpeShadow — hatched shadow wedge fills the material beneath the delamination, labeled UNEXAMINED, syncs with 'nothing behind it gets examined' (~7.5-9.5s). On-screen note 'STRONG ECHO AT DEPTH · BACK WALL GOES DARK' lands at 9.4s — keep lower-third clear for it.

c-scan-signatures.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Plan-view C-scan amplitude map of a flat rectangular laminate panel (this is the curriculum's requested 'C-scan signatures' diagram). Base art: panel outline with faint scan grid; above it a small gantry bar with twin squirter nozzles and thin water-jet lines. Reveals: (1) csgRaster — gantry sweeps left-to-right with a serpentine raster trail and the map area fills with a healthy uniform tone, syncs with 'automated squirters raster the part into a C-scan' (~0-3.5s); (2) csgDelam — a sharp-edged dead zone pops in (near-black blob with a crisp, hard outline), micro-label 'DELAM', syncs with 'delamination, a sharp-edged dead zone' (~3.5-5.8s); (3) csgPorosity — a mottled, diffuse, speckled patch of partial loss with soft edges, micro-label 'POROSITY', syncs with 'porosity, mottled loss' (~5.8-7.6s); (4) csgFilm — a small discrete geometric footprint (e.g. a clipped rectangle, the tell-tale shape of a backing-film scrap), micro-label 'FILM', syncs with 'a film inclusion, its discrete footprint' (~7.6-9.5s). The three signatures must be visually distinguishable at a glance: hard edge vs mottle vs discrete shape. Note 'SHARP EDGE · MOTTLED LOSS · DISCRETE SHAPE' lands at 9.1s — keep lower-third clear.

stress-concentration.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. One horizontal steel bar in cross-section spanning the frame, loaded in tension; cyan stress streamlines enter at left and exit right, reading like water flowing in a channel. Three zones left to right: (1) SOUND section — streamlines parallel and evenly spaced; (2) a smooth ROUND HOLE mid-bar — streamlines bow around it and bunch at its sides; (3) a SHARP, TIGHT surface crack — streamlines crowd violently together at the crack tip with a hot glow. Keep the bottom strip clear for the note chip at 7.6s. Scan sweep id stcscanG (~3.5s sweep). Beat narration (~8.5s): 'Stress flows like water. A round hole squeezes it to about three times average; a sharp crack crowds it far higher.' Reveals: stcFlow (draw, 0.8) — parallel streamlines stream through the sound zone as narration says stress flows like water; stcHole (fade, 2.4) — round hole appears and local lines bunch around it as the round hole is named; stcHoleTag (ping, 3.9) — small '≈3× AVG' chip at the hole edge on 'about three times average'; stcCrack (fade, 5.6) — sharp crack with violently crowded lines on 'a sharp crack'; stcCrackTip (ping, 6.9) — hot glow plus 'FAR HIGHER' tag at the crack tip on 'crowds it far higher'.

flux-leakage.brief:
600x340 cross-section of a ferromagnetic steel block carrying horizontal flux. Reveals: flFlux (draw, 0.0s — narration 'The limits') — five or six parallel flux lines flowing left-to-right through the section, subtle drift. flLeak (draw+ping, ~1.5s — narration 'only surface and near-surface flaws leak enough flux to show') — narrow surface-breaking crack interrupts the lines; flux bows out above the surface as a leakage loop with small N and S pole markers on the crack lips; particle cluster snaps to the leak and glows cyan; mono tag 'LEAKAGE FIELD'. flDeep (fade, ~3.8s — still under the 'only surface and near-surface' clause) — second flaw drawn deep in the section: flux deviates around it and re-closes inside the metal with no surface leakage, dim ghost styling, mono tag 'TOO DEEP — NO SURFACE LEAK'. Keep top strip clear: spec note 'NO MAGNETISM, NO LEAKAGE FIELD, NO TEST' lands at 7.9s. Canonical MT asset; intentionally mirrors lesson figure mt1-1-3-fig-a (anatomy of an indication) in animated form.
---
600x340 holographic navy/cyan cross-section of a magnetized steel bar (the crack field leak — canonical MT principle diagram). BASE ART (visible from start): horizontal steel bar in cross-section filling the lower two-thirds of frame, with a shallow surface-breaking crack notch at top-center kept subtle/dim until revealed; air gap above the bar. ANIMATABLE REVEALS, timed against the 24-word beat-3 narration 'The principle Hoke stumbled onto: magnetize the part, and a crack forces flux out of the surface. Particles pile up right at the leak.' (~9.5 s total; on-screen note fires at 9.0 s): [1] flxField at ~2.5 s, on 'magnetize the part' — parallel flux lines with arrowheads flowing left-to-right through the bar interior, continuous and evenly spaced. [2] flxLeak at ~5.0 s, on 'a crack forces flux out of the surface' — the crack notch brightens, and the flux lines nearest the surface deflect upward, bowing OUT of the bar and arcing through the air over the crack before re-entering on the far side; small mono label LEAKAGE FLUX with leader line to the arcing lines. [3] flxParticles at ~8.0 s, on 'particles pile up right at the leak' — a scatter of fine particle dots converges and stacks into a bright ridge bridging the crack mouth (the visible indication), with mono label INDICATION. Keep flux lines continuous everywhere except the deflection at the crack; do not draw magnetic poles or yoke hardware — this is the bare principle, not equipment.
---
Core teaching diagram: 2D cross-section of a steel bar carrying magnetic flux, with a tight surface-breaking crack (narrow V) at top center. Static base: bar cross-section with 5-6 horizontal flux lines flowing left-to-right inside the metal, thin air region above the surface, subtle ambient drift on the lines. Reveals timed to the 24-word diagram narration (~8.6s): flCrack (draw, at 1.0) — the V-shaped surface crack cuts into the bar, synced to 'Now add a crack'; flSqueeze (fade, at 2.2) — lower flux lines compress and crowd into the remaining ligament beneath the crack tip, synced to 'some flux squeezes beneath it'; flJump (fade, at 3.6) — one line crossing the thin air gap directly between the crack faces, synced to 'some jumps the gap'; flLeak (draw, at 5.0) — one or two lines bulging OUT of the part surface, arcing over the crack mouth through the air, and diving back in, synced to 'some escapes the surface: a leakage field'; flParticles (fade, at 6.8) + flPartPing (ping, at 7.0) — ferromagnetic particles piling into the leakage bulge and bridging the crack, forming a glowing indication clearly WIDER than the crack opening, synced to 'traps particles'. Do NOT label N/S poles or add other text — narration does not cover poles. Bottom strip clear for the 7.4s note chip (PARTICLES BRIDGE THE LEAK · CRACK MADE VISIBLE).
---
SHARED ASSET — also referenced by the diagram beats of mt1-1-1 ('crack forces flux out of the surface, particles pile up at the leak') and mt1-1-3 ('no magnetism, no leakage field, no test'); no SVG exists yet, so merge with any prior brief into ONE baked ~9.5 s timeline serving all three. 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. A horizontal magnetized steel bar (~500x150) with internal flux lines flowing left to right (animated dash flow); a narrow surface-breaking crack notched into the top edge at center. Reveals timed to mt1-2-2 beat 4 narration: 'Like poles repel, unlike attract. Crack a magnetized part and you create a pole pair that grabs particles into a crisp indication over the flaw.' — flxBar (fade 0.5) bar with flowing internal flux during 'Like poles repel, unlike attract'; flxCrack (fade 2.5) crack highlights and flux lines pinch and crowd beneath it on 'Crack a magnetized part'; flxPoles (fade 4.2) small N and S tags pop on the two crack edges on 'you create a pole pair'; flxLeak (draw 4.8) leakage field arcs bow out of the surface bridging the crack; flxParticles (fade 6.3) particle specks snap in from above onto the leak on 'grabs particles'; flxIndication (fade 7.5) specks consolidate into one bright crisp ridge directly over the crack with a subtle ping on 'a crisp indication over the flaw'. A few stray specks elsewhere drift off the bar — particles are held tightly only at the crack. Note overlay lands at 8.4.

flux-crossing-angle.brief: 600x340 top-down steel plate with flux lines flowing left-to-right; two identical short cracks: crack A vertical (flux crosses it at 90 degrees), crack B horizontal (parallel to flux). Reveals: fcaFlux (draw, 0.0s — narration 'Direction matters') — flux lines sweep on, gentle drift. fcaCross (ping+fade, ~1.4s — narration 'particles gather where flux crosses a crack') — crack A lights with a dense bright particle indication; small '90°' angle marker between crack and a flux line. fcaWindow (fade, ~3.4s — narration 'best at 45 to 90 degrees') — small protractor-arc badge beside crack A shading the 45-90 degree detection window, mono label '45–90°'. fcaParallel (fade, ~5.2s — narration 'parallel to the flux, it may show nothing') — crack B appears only as a dim ghost outline with NO particles, mono tag '0° — NO INDICATION'. Keep the bright-vs-ghost contrast stark; leave the note strip clear for the spec note at 7.2s. Reusable for every later direction/coverage lesson; distinct from circular-vs-longitudinal (field types), which mt1-3-x specs should request separately.

mt-wet-bench.brief: Wet horizontal MT bench station, side elevation, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan — title-panel asset (loops like pipe-scan). Scene: headstock contact plate at left with clamp lever, tailstock at right, a shaft clamped between them, magnetizing coil ring around the shaft mid-span, bath reservoir under the bed with recirculation hose and applicator nozzle resting on the rail, amperage readout panel above the headstock, faint booth-curtain line behind. Reveals (loop-internal timing, total loop ~4.4s): mwbscanG — standard scan sweep, ~3.5s sweep; mwbAmpPing — ping on the amperage readout at ~1.9s into the loop (title narration says 'low voltage but thousands of amperes'); mwbBathPing — ping at the bath nozzle at ~3.1s into the loop. Panel label overlay will read 'WET BENCH // HIGH AMPERAGE'.

arc-strike.brief: Close-up of a live prod-to-plate contact, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan — diagram asset for an ~8.5s beat. Base scene (visible at 0s): steel plate across the lower third, handheld prod leaning in from the right with cable trailing off-frame, small meter glyph upper-left showing CURRENT ON. Narration: 'Break a live connection at a head, clamp, or prod and you draw an arc — burning flesh, leaving arc strikes that condemn parts.' uv-lamp-fluorescent.brief:

Never stare into the lens, never run a cracked filter — and skin exposure adds up across a shift.' Reveals: uvlscanG — scan sweep ~4s; uvlBeam (fade, ~0.6s) — violet beam cone from filter to surface; uvlGlow (fade, ~0.9s) — single green-yellow fluorescent indication glowing on the part (narration: 'ultraviolet lamps demand respect'); uvlEye (fade, ~2.0s) — eye glyph at left with a sight line aimed INTO the lens; uvlEyeX (draw, ~2.5s) — red slash across that sight line (narration: 'never stare into the lens'); uvlCrack (draw, ~3.9s) — hairline crack across the filter disc; uvlCrackX (fade, ~4.4s) — red X tag by the cracked filter (narration: 'never run a cracked filter'); uvlArm (fade, ~5.6s) — forearm/hand outline at the beam edge; uvlDose (fade, ~6.1s) — small exposure bar filling tick by tick across shift-hour marks (narration: 'skin exposure adds up across a shift'). steel-to-atom.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan title panel: three-stage zoom from inspection scale to atomic scale, left to right. Left: steel pressure-vessel shell segment with a girth-weld seam. Two magnifier/zoom circles step rightward: first circle shows an ordered lattice of glowing iron atoms; second (largest, right third) shows a single atom — compact nucleus dot plus two thin orbit rings with small electrons that orbit continuously as the panel's ambient loop. Small mono scale tag '~10⁻¹⁰ m' under the final atom. Dashed zoom-cone lines connect stage to stage. Beat narration (~7.5s): 'Everything radiography does happens at a scale you can't see: the atom. Before sources and exposures, meet the cast.' Required reveals: staVessel (fade 0.4s — vessel + weld visible as panel opens), staZoomA (draw 1.4s — first zoom cone as 'a scale you can't see' begins), staLattice (fade 1.8s — iron-atom lattice), staZoomB (draw 2.9s — second zoom cone), staAtom (fade 3.3s — single atom lands exactly on the narration phrase 'the atom' ~3.4s), staScale (fade 5.2s — scale tag during 'before sources and exposures'). Electron orbit animation loops for the full beat.

atom-structure.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan teaching diagram: a carbon-12 atom matching lesson fig rt1-1-3-fig-b. Center-left: nucleus cluster of 6 protons (bright, small '+' glyphs) and 6 neutrons (dimmer, no glyph) packed together. Two concentric shell rings: K shell with 2 electrons, L shell with 4 electrons (tiny '−' glyphs). Three property callouts with leader lines: 'PROTON · +1 · ≈1 u' (to a proton), 'NEUTRON · 0 · ≈1 u' (to a neutron), 'ELECTRON · −1 · ≈1/1836 u' (to a K-shell electron). Keep bottom-center clear for the player's note chip at 8.6s ('ELECTRON MASS ≈ 1/1836 OF A PROTON'). Beat narration (~10s): 'In the nucleus: protons, one positive charge each, and neutrons, no charge. Both weigh about one mass unit. Electrons orbit in shells, negative and featherweight.' Required reveals, in narration order: atmNucleus (fade 0.5s — nucleus cluster appears on 'In the nucleus'), atmProtonPing (ping 1.1s — protons brighten on 'protons'), atmProtonLbl (fade 1.4s — proton callout during 'one positive charge each'), atmNeutronPing (ping 3.4s — neutrons highlight on 'and neutrons'), atmNeutronLbl (fade 3.7s — neutron callout during 'no charge'; its ≈1 u value pays off 'both weigh about one mass unit' ~5s), atmShells (draw 6.4s — K and L rings on 'Electrons orbit in shells'), atmElectrons (fade 6.9s — 6 electrons pop onto rings, then slow continuous orbit), atmElectronLbl (fade 7.5s — electron callout on 'negative and featherweight'). Reusable for rt1-1-4 (atomic structure / isotopes).

mt-bench.brief: Wet horizontal magnetic particle bench unit, side elevation, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Composition: left fixed headstock with circular contact plate, right movable tailstock on a rail, a steel shaft clamped horizontally between them; split coil on rails around the shaft mid-span; curved bath hose with nozzle aimed at the shaft; sump/tank under the bed with small recirculation arrows; compact control panel with ammeter dial at lower left. Used as the beat-1 TITLE PANEL (~10 s ambient loop, no word-level sync — same role as pipe-scan). No text labels inside the asset; the panel chrome supplies the label. Required animatable reveals: mbBath — looping bath stream pouring from the nozzle over the shaft with droplets returning to the sump, running continuously from 0 s; mbShot — ammeter needle kick plus a bright current-pulse glow along the shaft and both contact plates, firing first at ~1.5 s and looping about every 4 s; mbPing — a tight crack indication blooming on the shaft surface just after each mbShot pulse (particles gather while/just after current flows — continuous method), fading before the next loop.

hysteresis-loop.brief:
B-H hysteresis loop diagram, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Centered axes: horizontal axis labeled MAGNETIZING FORCE H, vertical axis labeled FLUX DENSITY B. One wide hysteresis loop (hard steel) plus a visibly narrower loop nested inside it (soft steel). A marker dot where the wide loop crosses the +B axis at H = 0, labeled RETENTIVITY. IMPORTANT SYNC CONSTRAINT: do NOT label saturation or coercive force anywhere — the narration does not cover them (keep the loop shape physically correct, just unlabeled elsewhere). Beat-3 narration (~9.4 s): 'First, the hysteresis loop. Cut the magnetizing force and retentivity remains. Hard steels keep it and allow the residual method — soft steels demand continuous.' Required reveals: hlAxes — axes and the two axis labels draw in at 0–1.0 s ('First, the hysteresis loop'); hlLoop — the wide loop traces around at 1.0–3.4 s; hlRetentivity — at 3.4–5.0 s ('cut the magnetizing force and retentivity remains') a highlight travels along the loop to the +B-axis crossing, the dot pings and the RETENTIVITY label pops in; hlHard — 5.0–7.0 s ('hard steels keep it and allow the residual method'): wide loop glows with small tag HARD STEEL · HOLDS FIELD · RESIDUAL; hlSoft — 7.0–9.2 s ('soft steels demand continuous'): narrow inner loop draws with tag SOFT STEEL · FORGETS · CONTINUOUS. The on-screen caption note RETENTIVITY DECIDES: RESIDUAL OR CONTINUOUS appears from the player at 8.4 s, so keep the lower band of the canvas clear.

B-H hysteresis loop teaching steel's magnetic memory; used as the mt2-3-2 title-beat panel, so keep markers bold and legible at panel scale. Layout: centered axes — horizontal H (magnetizing force), vertical B (flux density); one bold cyan major loop, plus a wider 'hardened steel' ghost loop behind it. Reveals timed vs narration 'The shot ends; steel remembers. / Retentivity is the field it keeps; / coercive force, the fight to remove it. / Hardened steel fights hardest.' (~8.5s): hysAxes (draw, 0.3s) B and H axes with labels; hysLoop (draw, 0.8s) main loop traced through saturation, landing as 'steel remembers' is spoken; hysRet (fade, 2.4s) glowing marker where the loop crosses the +B axis at H = 0, tag 'RETENTIVITY — FIELD KEPT'; hysCoer (fade, 4.5s) glowing marker where the loop crosses the −H axis at B = 0, tag 'COERCIVE FORCE — FORCE TO ERASE'; hysHard (fade, 6.7s) the wider outer loop brightens, tag 'HARDENED STEEL — WIDER LOOP, TIGHTER GRIP'.

circular-vs-longitudinal.brief:
Split comparison panel, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan, thin vertical divider. LEFT (CIRCULAR FIELD): steel bar between two contact heads (head shot), current arrow running along the bar axis, circular flux rings wrapping the bar circumference, a seam-like LONGITUDINAL flaw running along the bar axis; small label CIRCULAR FIELD. RIGHT (LONGITUDINAL FIELD): same bar inside a 3-turn coil, flux lines running along the bar axis, a TRANSVERSE crack across the bar; small label LONGITUDINAL FIELD. Beat-4 narration (~7.8 s): 'Next, the pairing: circular fields find longitudinal flaws; longitudinal fields find transverse. Full coverage is always two magnetizations, roughly perpendicular.' Required reveals: cvlCircular — 0.8–3.2 s ('circular fields find longitudinal flaws'): left half draws — current arrow, flux rings sweep around the bar, then the longitudinal flaw blooms with a particle-indication ping and tag FINDS LONGITUDINAL; cvlLongitudinal — 3.2–5.4 s ('longitudinal fields find transverse'): right half draws — coil turns, axial flux lines, transverse crack blooms with ping and tag FINDS TRANSVERSE; cvlTwoShots — 5.6–7.6 s ('two magnetizations, roughly perpendicular'): both flaw indications glow together and a crossed double-arrow badge marked ~90 DEG appears centered over the divider. Player note appears at 6.8 s in the lower band — keep it clear. This is the canonical asset matching lesson fig-b; future MT field-direction lessons should reuse it.
---
600x340 holographic navy/cyan split comparison of the two MT field directions; used here as an ambient TITLE-BEAT panel, but build it general-purpose for reuse. LEFT half: a straight current-carrying conductor (vertical bar) wrapped by circular field rings, current arrow along the conductor, small caption chip 'CIRCULAR'. RIGHT half: the same conductor bent into a 4-turn coil around a horizontal steel bar, with bright longitudinal flux arrows streaming straight down the bar's axis, caption chip 'LONGITUDINAL'. A subtle bend/morph arrow sweeps from the left wire to the right coil. Reveals sync to an ~8.5 s title narration: 'Bend that current-carrying wire into a coil and the field changes: flux now runs straight down the axis. That's longitudinal magnetization.' REVEALS: cvlWire (at ~0.6s, on 'Bend that current-carrying wire') — left straight wire and its circular rings draw in. cvlCoil (at ~1.8s, on 'into a coil') — bend arrow sweeps and the right coil turns draw in around the bar. cvlFlux (at ~3.2s, on 'flux now runs straight down the axis') — axial flux arrows stream through the coil and bar, left rings dim slightly, both caption chips fade in. After ~5s settle into a gentle ambient loop (slow flux drift) since it sits as a title panel for the remainder of the beat.

exposure-device-cutaway.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan side cutaway of a portable gamma exposure device (camera) with a guide tube running to a collimator at the work, matching lesson figure rt1-4-1-fig-b. Layout: device body left/center (~60% width) with rear drive-cable port and front guide-tube port visibly distinct; guide tube arcs right to a small collimator aimed at a pipe-weld section at far right.
