Right lane labeled 'EXPOSE, THEN KINK': beam icon then kink icon, ending in a swatch with a DARK crescent (darker than surround). Animatable reveal ids: cmtKink = sheet flex + crescent crease draw-on, 0-2.5 s ('bend a sheet and you crimp it'); cmtBefore = left lane draws and its LIGHT-crescent swatch pops, ~3.5 s (on 'kinked before exposure it images light'); cmtAfter = right lane draws and its DARK-crescent swatch pops, ~5.5 s (on 'after exposure, dark'); cmtMimic = small 'REAL FLAW?' tag pulsing beside the dark crescent while both crescents glow once, ~7 s (on 'crimps mimic real flaws'). Keep lower-center clear: the spec note 'LIGHT = BENT BEFORE · DARK = BENT AFTER' overlays at 7.2 s.

pulse-echo-blocks.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan block diagram of a pulse-echo flaw detector: five glass blocks plus the test part. Layout: CLOCK block far left-center. Two trigger lines fan out from the clock — one up-right to PULSER, one down-right to a TIME BASE / SWEEP block sitting under a DISPLAY screen at far right (display starts as an empty graticule). PULSER connects to TRANSDUCER (probe coupled atop a steel part slab bottom-center, small flaw inside the part). Echo path returns from the probe to a RECEIVER block (between transducer and display, with a small-in / large-out amplitude glyph), then into the display. Keep bottom-center clear for the player's note chip at 8.4s ('SAME INSTANT → SCREEN POSITION = TIME'). Reveals timed to the beat-2 narration 'The clock fires the pulser and sweep at the same instant, so screen position is time. The transducer converts, the receiver amplifies, the display draws.' (~9.5s): pebClock (fade 0.6) clock block lights with a tick pulse — 'the clock'; pebTrigPulser (draw 1.3) trigger line to pulser, pulser block lights — 'fires the pulser'; pebTrigSweep (draw 1.9) trigger line to time base/sweep block — 'and sweep'; pebSyncPing (ping 2.7) both trigger lines flash simultaneously — 'at the same instant'; pebTimeAxis (fade 4.1) display horizontal axis lights with time ticks and a small 'TIME →' tag — 'screen position is time'; pebShot (draw 5.6) pulse travels probe into part, echo returns from the flaw — 'the transducer converts'; pebReceiver (fade 6.9) receiver block lights, small→large signal glyph — 'the receiver amplifies'; pebTrace (draw 7.8) A-scan trace draws on the display (initial pulse, flaw echo, back-wall echo) — 'the display draws.'

edac-swept-gain.brief: Electronic distance-amplitude correction (swept gain / TCG), 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Static base: left third = test-block cross-section with a probe on top and three IDENTICAL side-drilled-hole markers (same diameter) at shallow, mid, and deep depth; right two-thirds = A-scan grid (horizontal sweep = time/depth, vertical = percent of full screen height) with a dashed evaluation level at about 80 percent FSH. Animatable reveals in narration order (beat narration about 9.4 s: 'Identical reflectors echo weaker when deeper: beam spread and attenuation. Electronic distance amplitude correction adds gain rising with time: equal reflectors, equal height, anywhere.'): (1) tcgEchoes at ~0.8 s, during 'echo weaker when deeper' — three echo peaks at increasing sweep positions with clearly FALLING heights (about 80/50/30 percent FSH), thin leader lines pairing each hole to its echo, dashed falling envelope through the peaks; (2) tcgCauses at ~2.6 s, during 'beam spread and attenuation' — faint widening beam cone through the block, small labels BEAM SPREAD and ATTENUATION; (3) tcgRamp at ~4.6 s, during 'adds gain rising with time' — rising gain-vs-time ramp line along the bottom of the A-scan labeled GAIN RISES WITH TIME; (4) tcgFlat at ~7.4 s, during 'equal reflectors, equal height, anywhere' — the three echoes boosted to EQUAL height on the flat evaluation level, falling envelope fades out, label ONE FLAT LEVEL. Keep the lower third clear for the spec's note overlay at 8.5 s ('EQUAL REFLECTORS READ EQUAL HEIGHT').

mt-five-question-plan.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan decision-flow diagram. Far left: wireframe silhouette of a splined shaft on a bench pedestal, tagged UNKNOWN PART. Center: a left-to-right chain of five numbered hexagonal nodes on a glowing path. Far right: a technique-sheet card titled TECHNIQUE with five empty ruled lines. Animatable reveals, fired in this order against the narration 'Five questions, in order. What is the part: alloy, shape, condition. What current. Which directions. What sequence. And how much field.': mfqPart = node 1 lights, label THE PART with sub-line ALLOY / SHAPE / CONDITION (at 'what is the part: alloy, shape, condition'); mfqCurrent = node 2 lights, label CURRENT TYPE (at 'what current'); mfqDirections = node 3 lights, label FIELD DIRECTIONS (at 'which directions'); mfqSequence = node 4 lights, label SEQUENCE (at 'what sequence'); mfqFlux = node 5 lights, label FLUX DENSITY (at 'and how much field'); mfqSheet = the technique card's five ruled lines fill in and the card glows (fires last, with the on-screen note, about one second before beat end). Keep node sub-labels minimal: only node 1 carries a sub-line, since only alloy/shape/condition are narrated in this beat.

density-mirror.brief: DIAGRAM (600x340, holographic navy/cyan): two radiograph frames side by side on a glowing viewer strip — mirrored density failures. Narration (~10 s): 'Density first. Too dark: too much exposure, or developer run hot. Too light? Mirror the list — iridium one ninety-two halves about every seventy-four days.' Reveals: dmrFrames at 0.5 — two empty film frames on the viewer glow; dmrDark at 0.9 — LEFT film floods near-black (weld image barely visible), mono tag 'TOO DARK'; dmrDarkCauses at 1.8 — two cause chips under left film: 'OVEREXPOSURE' and 'DEVELOPER HOT' (sync: 'too much exposure, or developer run hot' ~1.5-3.8 s); dmrLight at 4.2 — RIGHT film renders thin and pale, tag 'TOO LIGHT' (sync 'Too light?' ~4.2 s); dmrMirror at 4.9 — mirrored double-headed arrow between the two films (sync 'mirror the list' ~4.8-5.6 s); dmrDecay at 6.2 — inset bottom-right: Ir-192 activity-vs-time curve stepping down by halves, x-axis ticks every 74 days (sync 'iridium one ninety-two halves...' ~6-9.5 s); dmrHalfChip at 7.8 — chip on the curve: 'x 1/2 @ ~74 D'. Renderer note appears at 9.2, keep lower-third clear.

film-artifact-gallery.brief: DIAGRAM (600x340, holographic navy/cyan): one wide dark film strip with a faint weld image band, four artifact signatures revealed left-to-right in narration order. Narration (~10 s): 'Artifacts are false images. Crimp before exposure prints light crescents; after, dark. Static draws dark branching lines; air bells in the developer leave light spots.' Reveals: artStrip at 0.5 — base film strip with faint weld image; artCrimpLight at 2.0 — cluster of sharp LIGHT crescent (half-moon kink) marks, mono tag 'CRIMP - BEFORE EXPOSURE' (sync ~1.8-3.6 s); artCrimpDark at 3.9 — matching DARK crescents, tag 'CRIMP - AFTER' (sync 'after, dark' ~3.9-4.6 s); artStatic at 5.2 — dark branching lightning/tree-shaped filament lines, tag 'STATIC' (sync ~5.2-7.0 s); artAirBells at 7.4 — scattered small LIGHT round spots, tag 'AIR BELLS' (sync ~7.4-9.2 s). Crescents must read as crisp kinked half-moons (light vs dark clearly opposite), static as fine branched filaments, air bells as circular light dots. Renderer note appears at 9.2, keep lower-third clear; only these four signatures — no extra artifacts.

linac-vault.brief: Title-panel scene, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan: cutaway industrial radiography vault. Thick concrete walls in section (hatched), maze-style entry at left, small remote-operation console with operator silhouette OUTSIDE the wall. Inside: a linear accelerator head mounted on an overhead gantry, aimed down at a thick steel casting on rollers; warning beacon on the wall. No people inside the vault. Animatable reveals (narration is 18 words, ~7.5s: 'When steel gets inches thick, X ray tubes and even cobalt sixty fall short. Megavolt machines take over.'): vltRoom at 0.0s — base vault cutaway, concrete walls, maze entry; vltGantry at 0.8s — linac head + gantry + steel casting on rollers fade in (the big rig is the visual subject while narration says tubes and cobalt fall short); vltBeam at 5.6s — cyan beam cone fires from the head through the casting, synced to 'Megavolt machines take over'; vltWarn at 6.5s — warning beacon glows and the exterior remote console highlights, holds to beat end. Uppercase mono micro-labels only (e.g. CONCRETE, REMOTE CONSOLE).

linac-vs-betatron.brief: Split-comparison teaching diagram, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan, left vs right, thin center divider. LEFT (LINAC): horizontal straight evacuated waveguide with repeated RF cavity segments; a sine microwave wave runs along it with 2-3 electron dots riding a crest (surfer feel, motion streaks), accelerating toward a target block at the right end of the guide; from the target, a WIDE bright beam cone punches through a thick steel step block. Tag: HIGH OUTPUT / SHORT EXPOSURE. RIGHT (BETATRON): doughnut-shaped toroidal tube between magnet pole pieces (rising-field arrows), electron dots orbiting on an arrowed circular path, then flicked out to a small target; the target shows a TINY glowing focal spot with magnifier callout SMALL FOCAL SPOT and a mini inset of a crisp edge profile. Tag: SHARP IMAGE / LOWER OUTPUT. Animatable reveals (narration is 25 words, ~10s: 'The linac surfs electrons on microwaves down a waveguide — huge output, short exposures. The betatron whirls them in a magnetic ring — tiny spot, sharp image.'): lvbLinac at 0.3s — left waveguide structure + LINAC label; lvbSurf at 1.5s — microwave sine + electrons riding the crest animate ('surfs electrons on microwaves down a waveguide'); lvbOutput at 3.8s — wide beam cone + steel block + HIGH OUTPUT tag ('huge output, short exposures'); lvbBetatron at 5.5s — right doughnut tube, magnet poles, orbiting electrons + BETATRON label ('whirls them in a magnetic ring'); lvbSharp at 8.4s — tiny focal spot callout + crisp-edge inset + SHARP IMAGE tag ('tiny spot, sharp image'). Player overlays the beat note at 9.0s, keep bottom-center strip clear. Uppercase mono labels.

piezo-effect.brief: Split diagram of the two piezoelectric effects, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Left and right halves each show the same thin disc cross-section with electrode faces. LEFT = TRANSMIT (inverse effect): pulser voltage-spike symbol feeding the electrodes; disc drawn with dashed expanded/contracted outlines plus thickness-change arrows; wavefronts radiating away. RIGHT = RECEIVE (direct effect): incoming echo arcs with pressure arrows squeezing the disc; small millivolt waveform appearing across the electrodes. Reveals vs the 21-word (~8.6 s) narration 'Voltage in, the disc changes thickness — the inverse effect transmits. Echo pressure in, the disc makes voltage — the direct effect receives': pzoSpike (draw 0.5s) voltage pulse entering the left disc on 'Voltage in'; pzoSwell (fade 1.5s) dashed thickness-change outlines + arrows on 'changes thickness'; pzoTxWave (draw 3.0s) outgoing wavefronts + caption INVERSE → TRANSMIT on 'the inverse effect transmits'; pzoEcho (draw 4.9s) incoming echo arcs pressing the right disc on 'Echo pressure in'; pzoVolt (fade 6.0s) millivolt signal across the electrodes on 'makes voltage'; pzoRxLabel (fade 7.1s) caption DIRECT → RECEIVE on the final phrase. Keep left-then-right reveal order to match narration.

thickness-frequency.brief: Element thickness vs resonant frequency, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Left: large element cross-section containing a half-wavelength standing wave with bracket 't = λ/2'. Center: formula panel 'f = v ÷ 2t' with small caption 'v ≈ 4,000 m/s in PZT'. Right: three discs in a row, thick to thin, labeled '2.25 MHz · 0.89 mm', '5 MHz · 0.40 mm', '10 MHz · 0.20 mm', the thinnest carrying a small warning tag FRAGILE (values from the lesson's worked examples). Reveals vs the 24-word (~9.8 s) narration 'The element resonates when it's half a wavelength thick: frequency equals velocity divided by twice the thickness. Thinner element, higher frequency — and more fragile': thfDisc (fade 0.5s) the cross-section appears on 'The element resonates'; thfWave (draw 1.6s) standing wave + λ/2 bracket on 'half a wavelength thick'; thfFormula (fade 4.0s) the f = v ÷ 2t panel as the formula is spoken; thfTrio (fade 6.9s) the thick-to-thin disc row on 'Thinner element, higher frequency'; thfFragile (ping 8.8s) FRAGILE tag pings on 'more fragile'.

darkroom-layout.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan darkroom cutaway, used as a TITLE-BEAT side panel (panelLabel: DRY SIDE // WET SIDE). One room split by a center divider: left DRY SIDE with loading bench, film box, cassette, and hanger rack; right WET SIDE with four processing tanks in a row; a warm amber safelight lamp high on the wall (the only non-cyan accent) casting a soft glow cone; light-trapped door at far left. Ambient loop: gentle safelight glow pulse. Narration over this beat (~9.5s): 'Every radiograph begins as raw film in someone's hands. A sloppy darkroom ruins a perfect exposure, and the damage mimics real flaws.' Reveals: drkRoom at 0.5 (room shell, divider, door); drkSafelight at 1.0 (amber lamp + glow cone); drkFilm at 2.2 (a film sheet held by one edge above the bench — syncs 'raw film in someone's hands'); drkDryLabel at 4.4 (DRY SIDE tag over the bench); drkWetLabel at 5.0 (WET SIDE tag over the tanks); drkGhostFlaw at 8.0 (small ghost-radiograph chip in a corner with a faint crescent crimp mark flickering on — syncs 'the damage mimics real flaws').

tube-shift-parallax.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan side-view geometry of the double-exposure tube-shift (parallax) depth method. Static base: steel wall cross-section (~4 units thick) resting on a film/cassette strip at the bottom; one round pore-style flaw embedded high in the wall; vertical dimension rail at right labeled 'SFD' from focal-spot height down to the film plane. Beat narration (~9.2 s, 24 words): 'Tube shift: two half exposures on one film, moving only the source. Every flaw prints twice. The higher it floats, the bigger the jump.' Required animatable reveals: tspSetup (0.0 s, with 'two half exposures on one film') = source focal spot position 1 at upper left, thin ray through the flaw printing image dot 1 on the film, small '1/2 EXPOSURE' tag; tspShift (2.6 s, 'moving only the source') = focal spot position 2 to the right with horizontal arrow labeled 'a · KNOWN SHIFT', lock glyphs on part and cassette showing nothing else moves, second '1/2 EXPOSURE' tag; tspPair (4.5 s, 'Every flaw prints twice') = ray from position 2 through the same flaw printing image dot 2, bracket between dots labeled 'b · IMAGE SHIFT', flaw height labeled 'h ABOVE FILM'; tspCompare (6.6 s, 'The higher it floats, the bigger the jump') = two ghost flaws for contrast, one just above the film with a nearly coincident image pair tagged 'BARELY MOVES', one near the source-side surface with a wide pair tagged 'MOVES MOST'. Keep labels short; the height-to-shift proportionality must read at a glance. A note banner 'THE HIGHER THE FLAW, THE BIGGER THE JUMP' overlays at 8.4 s (player-rendered, leave lower band clear).

parallax-depth-formula.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan formula-and-worked-example card for tube-shift depth arithmetic. Layout: left half formula stack with a tiny tube-shift geometry thumbnail keying the symbols; right half worked example; lower right a small wall sketch. Beat narration (~9.6 s, 25 words): 'One line: height equals image shift, times source to film distance, divided by source shift plus image shift. Three inches up. Excavate from the top.' Required animatable reveals: pdmFormula (0.5 s, as the terms are spoken) = large equation h = b x SFD / (a + b), legend h = HEIGHT ABOVE FILM, b = IMAGE SHIFT, SFD = SOURCE TO FILM DISTANCE, a = SOURCE SHIFT; if feasible glow terms in narration order b, SFD, a; pdmPlug (5.8 s, end of the formula sentence) = lesson's numbers plug in: SFD 30 IN, a 4.5 IN, b 0.5 IN, then (0.5 x 30) / (4.5 + 0.5) = 15 / 5; pdmResult (7.0 s, 'Three inches up') = result '3.0 IN ABOVE FILM' highlighted and the flaw dot lighting up in the wall sketch (4.0 IN wall resting on cassette, flaw 3.0 above film); pdmVerdict (8.2 s, 'Excavate from the top') = arrow from the source-side (top) surface down to the flaw tagged 'EXCAVATE FROM TOP · 1.0 IN'. Arithmetic must be literally legible; numbers are the lesson's worked example. Note banner 'H = B x SFD / (A + B)' overlays at 8.8 s (player-rendered, leave lower band clear).

pipe-notch-vpath.brief: Pipe cross-section as a full ring: cyan OD circle, cyan ID circle, navy wall between, with 'OD' and 'ID' surface labels. A small angle-beam wedge sits on the OD near top-left. From the wedge a refracted shear-wave beam travels inside the wall as straight chord legs in a V-path moving clockwise around the circumference: leg 1 from the entry point down to an ID point, leg 2 bouncing back out to an OD point. A small rectangular ID notch (machined nick in the bore surface) sits exactly at the leg-1 bounce point; a matching OD notch (nick in the outer surface) sits at the leg-2 arrival point. Bounce-point tags 'HALF-V' (at ID notch) and 'FULL-V' (at OD notch) stay hidden until their reveals. Reveals: pnvBeam — the V-path draws leg by leg from the wedge, starting ~0.5s, both legs complete by ~5.0s, synced to narration 'a shear wave chasing the circumference... the beam vees through the wall'; pnvIdNotch — ID notch pings/highlights with its HALF-V tag at ~6.3s on 'inside notch at half-vee'; pnvOdNotch — OD notch pings with its FULL-V tag at ~7.9s on 'outside at full-vee'. After all reveals, loop a subtle pulse traveling the V-path. Beat runs ~9.5s; the scene note appears at 8.4s, so keep the lower band clear for it.

cassette-sandwich.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan edge-on cutaway of a loaded film cassette, layers slightly exploded (mirrors lesson fig rt1-6-3-fig-a): light-tight shell outline, low-absorption front face, FRONT LEAD SCREEN tagged 0.005 IN, double-emulsion film sheet, BACK LEAD SCREEN tagged 0.010 IN, felt/spring pressure backing. Used as the beat-1 title panel, so it should settle into an ambient loop. Required reveals: casStack (at 0s, the exploded layer stack assembles and labels fade in as narration opens 'Industrial X ray film captures only about one percent'); casBeam (~2.5s, faint photon streaks pour straight through the film layer with a small '~1% CAPTURED' tag on the film, syncing with 'one percent of the radiation that reaches it'); casFoils (~6s, both lead foils glow cyan and their thickness tags brighten, landing on 'Lead screens exist to claw that wasted energy back').

intensifying-factor.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan teaching diagram for beat 3. Left: formula card 'IF = EXPOSURE WITHOUT SCREENS / EXPOSURE WITH SCREENS' visualized as a tall BARE exposure bar and a short WITH SCREENS bar feeding a ratio chip (no specific numbers; the worked 12/4 example is not narrated). Right: two screen cards side by side: LEAD FOIL card with a crisp mini-radiograph and badge 'IF 2-4'; FLUORESCENT card with phosphor-glow styling and badge 'IF 10-100'. Required reveals: ifxFormula (at 0s, bars and formula draw on while narration defines 'exposure without screens divided by exposure with screens'); ifxLead (~4s, lead card and IF 2-4 badge light up on 'Lead gives two to four'); ifxFluoro (~6s, fluorescent card and IF 10-100 badge light up on 'Ten to one hundred'); ifxBlur (~8s, the fluorescent card's mini-radiograph blooms soft with sideways light rays and mottle grain while the lead card stays crisp, on 'spreading light costs sharpness', arriving just before the on-screen note 'SPREADING LIGHT COSTS SHARPNESS' at noteAt 8.2).

echo-dynamics.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. LEFT ~2/3: top-down PLAN VIEW of a butt weld — vertical weld seam band with cap-ripple hatching center-left, plate around it, an angle-beam probe (rounded-rect shoe, cable stub, index-point tick) on the plate right of the seam, faint beam wedge from the index point to a glowing indication dot inside the weld zone. This is the static base (no reveal id). RIGHT ~1/3: two small stacked A-scan windows labeled PLANAR (top) and VOLUMETRIC (bottom), baselines empty at start; keep labels to those two words only — the beat's caption note ('MIRROR COLLAPSES OFF-ANGLE · SCATTERER HOLDS') lands at 8.2s and must not be duplicated. Beat narration (~9.5s): 'Hold the indication and work the probe — traverse, lateral, orbit, swivel. A mirror collapses as the angle changes; a scatterer barely cares.' Reveals, in this exact order: edyTraverse (fade, ~1.6s) cyan double-headed arrow through the probe PERPENDICULAR to the weld + tiny tag TRAVERSE (probe toward/away from weld). edyLateral (fade, ~2.5s) double-headed arrow PARALLEL to the weld + tag LATERAL (traces length). edyOrbit (draw, ~3.3s) dashed arc centered on the indication dot at constant radius, arrowheads both ends, + tag ORBIT (constant sound path). edySwivel (fade, ~4.1s) small circular rotation arrow on the probe body itself + tag SWIVEL (twist in place). edyMirror (fade, ~5.2s) top window: one tall sharp spike with a dim ghost spike collapsed near baseline and a small down-arrow between them — the mirror lost as incidence leaves normal. edyScatter (fade, ~7.2s) bottom window: cluster of low narrow peaks with a subtle steady-hold underline — barely changes with angle.

flaw-orientation.brief: Side-by-side detectability comparison (600x340, holographic navy/cyan, scan sweep id foScanG). Both halves: small source dot at top, vertical beam rays passing down through a horizontal steel-plate cross-section, film strip below the plate. LEFT (flat case): paper-thin cyan lamination lying horizontal at mid-thickness, perpendicular to the beam — the metal path along the beam is essentially unchanged, so the film below stays uniform/blank. RIGHT (aligned case): the same tight planar separation drawn edge-on (vertical, parallel to the beam) — the ray running along the flaw plane crosses less metal and prints one sharp dark line on the film directly beneath. Reveals vs the beat-3 narration ('Radiography only sees a flaw that changes the metal path.' 0-3.5s | 'A flat lamination hides from a straight beam;' 3.5-6.5s | 'aligned, it prints a sharp dark line.' 6.5-10s): foBeams (fade, 0.6) beam rays switch on in both halves; foPathLbl (fade, 2.0) small caption IMAGE = CHANGE IN METAL PATH centered between halves; foLam (draw, 3.8) horizontal mid-thickness lamination on left plate; foLamPing (ping, 4.0) on the lamination; foFilmClean (fade, 5.2) left film stays uniform with CLEAN FILM tag; foAligned (draw, 6.6) vertical beam-parallel separation on right plate; foDarkLine (draw, 7.6) sharp dark line on right film with DARK LINE tag; foLinePing (ping, 7.9) on the dark line. Keep the lower third visually quiet after 8.6s — the spec's on-screen note (FLAT TO BEAM → NO IMAGE · ALIGNED → DARK LINE) lands there.

lof-ip-planes.brief: Single-V butt weld cross-section, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan: two plate halves with a V groove (bevels ~30 degrees off vertical each side), multi-pass weld bead drawn dim inside, far surface at bottom. Two planar flaws only: a lack-of-fusion seam lying flat along the LEFT bevel face at mid-wall, and a short vertical incomplete-penetration slot on the root centerline (unfused root face forming a small corner with the far surface). Narration (~9.5s): 'Lack of fusion hugs the bevel it never melted — about thirty degrees off vertical. Incomplete penetration leaves a vertical slot at the root centerline.' Reveals: fplGroove (fade, 0.8s) groove outline + bevel edges brighten while passes dim, as narration opens; fplLof (draw, 1.6s) bright LOF seam tracing the left bevel face, synced to 'hugs the bevel it never melted'; fplArc (fade, 4.2s) dashed vertical reference line + angle arc labeled '~30 DEG OFF VERTICAL' at the LOF plane, synced to 'about thirty degrees off vertical'; fplIp (draw, 6.0s) vertical unfused slot at the root centerline, synced to 'incomplete penetration'; fplVert (fade, 7.6s) dashed weld centerline through the slot + tag 'VERTICAL / ROOT CENTERLINE', synced to 'vertical slot at the root centerline'; fplBoth (ping, 8.4s) both flaw planes pulse together just before the player note chip lands at 8.6s. Keep the lower third clear for the note chip.

immersion-ascan.brief: Immersion A-scan screen, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan: dark scope panel with faint grid and horizontal time base. Far left, a tall initial pulse. Then a flat quiet stretch with a bracket below labeled WATER PATH. Then the tallest, brightest spike: the front-surface echo, labeled FS. Right of FS, a faintly shaded zone labeled IN THE METAL containing a mid-height flaw echo and a tall back-wall echo labeled BW near the right edge. Keep bottom-center clear for the player's note chip at 8.2s. Required reveals (diagram-beat narration, ~9.5s): iaPulse (fade ~0.7s) initial pulse as narration opens 'Sound crosses water before metal' (~0-2s); iaWater (fade ~1.7s) the water-path bracket and label; iaFront (draw ~3.6s) the tall FS echo, syncing 'the first big echo is the part's front surface' (~2.5-6s); iaMetal (fade ~6.2s) shaded metal zone right of FS, syncing 'everything inside the metal' (~6-7.5s); iaFlaw (draw ~7.0s) flaw echo inside the zone; iaBack (draw ~7.8s) back-wall echo, syncing 'appears to its right' (~7.5-9.5s).

residual-field.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. A machined steel shaft floating center, the moment after the magnetizing current is cut. Reveals timed to the title-beat narration (~8s): rfPart — the shaft with faint internal flux loops, visible from the start; rfSwitch — a supply cable and switch glyph at left that flicks OFF and goes dark at 'switch the current off' (~0.5–2s); rfFlux — the internal flux lines stay lit and pulse brighter at 'the magnetism stays' (~2–4.5s); rfLabel — a caption tag reading RESIDUAL MAGNETISM appearing beside the shaft at 'residual magnetism' (~4.5–6s); rfNeedle — a small pocket field-indicator inset near the shaft end whose pointer swings off zero and holds there at 'hard steels hold it tightest' (~6–8s). After all reveals, loop a gentle shimmer on the flux lines. Used as the title-beat panel, label 'RESIDUAL FIELD // SHAFT'.

demag-decay.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan, split layout teaching the reverse-and-diminish principle. LEFT: applied magnetizing force vs time — an alternating waveform whose amplitude decays to zero. RIGHT: a B–H plane where hysteresis loops spiral inward to the origin (matches lesson fig 'Diminishing hysteresis loops during demagnetization'). Reveals timed to narration (~10s beat, note fires at 7.8s): ddAxes — both axis frames drawn at 'Every method works the same way' (0–2s); ddWave — the alternating waveform draws left to right, polarity flipping every cycle, at 'keep reversing the field' (~2–4s); ddEnvelope — a dashed decay envelope clamps the waveform amplitude down toward zero at 'while it shrinks to zero' (~4–5.5s); ddStart — a marker on the first peak labeled START ≥ ORIGINAL FIELD at 'start at or above the original force' (~5.5–7.5s); ddLoops — on the B–H plane, loops re-trace smaller and smaller, collapsing to a final dot at the origin, at 'then taper' (~7.5–9.5s), still settling as the on-screen note appears.

contoured-shoe.brief: Problem-left / cure-right comparison of an angle-beam wedge on a pipe OD (600x340, holographic navy/cyan). LEFT: large pipe outside-diameter arc across the lower third; flat-faced wedge resting on it so the face touches along ONE tangent line, hatched air gaps under heel and toe. RIGHT (dim/inactive until final reveal): same wedge with face contoured to the pipe radius, mating fully. Animatable reveals vs the 25-word beat-2 narration 'A flat wedge touches a pipe on one line: weak coupling, a rocking probe, a wandering exit point. Gap over half a millimeter? Contour it.' (~10 s @ ~2.5 w/s): cshLine (0 s, 'touches a pipe on one line') base scene, contact line glows cyan, air-gap hatching visible; cshWeakBeam (~3.8 s, 'weak coupling') thin dim refracted beam enters the wall only at the contact line and fades — most of the face transmits nothing; cshRock (~4.8 s, 'a rocking probe, a wandering exit point') rocking arc-arrows over the wedge, exit-point marker ghosts fore/aft and the refracted-angle line ghosts between two positions; cshGap (~7.4 s, 'Gap over half a millimeter?') dimension callout at the heel gap reading '0.5 mm (0.020 in.)' with a limit tick — numeric callout only, since the spec note 'GAP OVER 0.5 MM (0.020 IN)? CONTOUR IT' lands at 8.5 s; cshContoured (~9.4 s, 'Contour it.') right side lights up: contoured face mates fully, even couplant film line, bright solid beam, exit-point marker pinned steady.

pipe-chord.brief: End-on pipe cross-section showing circumferential-scan chord geometry (600x340, holographic navy/cyan). Pipe annulus with OD circle and ID (bore) circle labeled 'OD'/'ID', wall band shaded, proportions like 4-inch schedule 40 (ID/OD ≈ 0.89); small angle wedge on the OD at upper left aiming the beam around the circumference. Animatable reveals vs the 22-word beat-3 narration 'Around the pipe, the beam is a chord. If sine theta exceeds I D over O D, it never reaches the bore.' (~9 s): pchSection (0 s, 'Around the pipe') cross-section + labels + wedge; pchChord (~1.5 s, 'the beam is a chord') straight chord ray labeled '60°' draws from the entry point across the curved wall and strikes the bore at a glancing angle, small angle markers showing the beam-to-wall angle steepening inward, tag '≈75° AT BORE' at the strike point (lesson worked example, 4-in sch 40); pchMiss (~3.6 s, 'If sine theta exceeds I D over O D') second steeper chord labeled '70°' draws from the same entry, stays within the outer wall, visibly clears the ID circle without touching it and returns to the OD, bore pulses faintly to show it is never touched; pchLimit (~6.0 s, 'it never reaches the bore') the limiting chord drawn exactly tangent to the ID tagged 'LIMIT: SIN θ = ID ÷ OD', and the 70° path dims with a short 'MISS' tag. Spec note 'SIN θ ≤ ID ÷ OD — OR IT NEVER REACHES THE BORE' appears at 7.4 s, so keep on-asset tags short to complement, not repeat.

rayleigh-wave.brief: 600x340 side cutaway: steel block fills the lower two-thirds, top surface line prominent. Far left, a steep surface-wave wedge probe (element on the angled back face), incident ray visibly steeper than any shear wedge, small angle arc labeled 'PAST 2ND CRITICAL'. From the wedge toe a Rayleigh wave travels right ALONG the top surface: a chain of small rolling elliptical particle-motion loops riding the surface line, loop size decaying with depth. Beneath the surface a shaded cyan skin band with a right-edge depth bracket '≈ 1 λ DEEP'. At right, a sharp surface-breaking crack notch interrupting the surface with a bold echo arrow returning left toward the probe. Mid-path, a stylized fingertip pressing the surface; the wave beyond it drawn collapsed/dotted. Reveals synced to narration ('What survives past the second critical angle grips the surface: a Rayleigh wave, energy within about one wavelength. Surface cracks echo; a fingertip absorbs it.', ~9.6s): ryWedge (fade 0.6) wedge + 'PAST 2ND CRITICAL' arc — 'past the second critical angle'; ryWave (draw 2.4) rolling surface wave traveling right — 'grips the surface: a Rayleigh wave'; ryDepth (fade 4.8) one-wavelength skin band + '≈ 1 λ DEEP' bracket — 'energy within about one wavelength'; ryCrack (fade 6.6) crack notch + strong return echo arrow — 'surface cracks echo'; ryFinger (fade 8.2) fingertip damping the path, wave beyond it collapsing — 'a fingertip absorbs it'. Keep bottom-center clear for the player note chip at 8.6s.

lamb-modes.brief: 600x340 two stacked half-panels, each a long slender plate in side view (thickness clearly only a few wave squiggles), each with a dashed centerline; left-edge thickness bracket 'A FEW λ THICK'. TOP panel labeled 'SYMMETRIC (S) — BREATHES': the two plate surfaces bulge and pinch as mirror images about the centerline, so thickness periodically swells and necks along the length (centerline stays straight). BOTTOM panel labeled 'ASYMMETRIC (A) — FLEXES': both surfaces undulate together, the whole plate snaking, centerline waving with it. Right side: small inset dispersion plot, x-axis 'f × t', y-axis 'VELOCITY', two clearly non-constant curving mode lines labeled S and A. Reveals synced to narration ('Only a few wavelengths thick, the whole plate vibrates: Lamb waves. Symmetric modes breathe, asymmetric flex, and velocity tracks frequency times thickness: dispersion.', ~9s): lbPlate (fade 0.5) both plain plate outlines + centerlines + 'A FEW λ THICK' bracket — 'only a few wavelengths thick, the whole plate vibrates'; lbSym (draw 2.4) symmetric breathing waveform + S label — 'symmetric modes breathe'; lbAsym (draw 4.4) asymmetric snaking waveform + A label — 'asymmetric flex'; lbCurve (fade 6.2) dispersion-curve inset — 'velocity tracks frequency times thickness: dispersion'. Keep bottom-center clear for the player note chip at 8.0s.

echo-vs-shadow.brief: 600x340 vertical split panel contrasting the two transmission methods. LEFT: block cross-section with ONE probe on the top face; downward pulse arrow to a small mid-depth flaw and a return echo arrow back up to the same probe; below the block a mini A-scan strip (initial pulse, flaw echo, back-wall echo) with an arrow labeled 'TIME → DEPTH'. RIGHT: block with a transmitter probe on the top face and a receiver probe aligned directly opposite on the bottom face, straight beam crossing the thickness; a flaw mid-path casts a shaded shadow cone over the receiver; beside it a received-signal level bar falling from a high reference line to low with a bold down arrow labeled 'SIGNAL DROPS'. Reveals synced to narration ('Pulse echo: one probe sends and listens; arrival time gives depth, from one side. Through transmission: two aligned probes; a flaw's shadow drops the signal.', ~9.6s): evSplit (fade 0.4) center divider + left panel title 'PULSE ECHO'; evProbe (draw 0.9) single probe + down pulse + return echo — 'one probe sends and listens'; evScan (fade 2.9) mini A-scan + 'TIME → DEPTH' — 'arrival time gives depth, from one side'; evPair (draw 5.2) right panel title 'THROUGH TRANSMISSION' + transmitter/receiver pair + crossing beam — 'two aligned probes'; evDrop (fade 7.3) flaw + shadow cone + falling signal bar — 'a flaw's shadow drops the signal'. Keep bottom-center clear for the player note chip at 8.6s.

dac-curve.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Left ~55%: cross-section of a basic calibration block (thickness T) with three identical side-drilled holes at T/4, T/2, and 3T/4 depth (labeled), straight-beam probe coupled on the top surface. Right ~45%: A-scan grid with a faint 80% FSH gridline and a small gain readout (e.g. 'REF 46 dB'). Base state: block + labeled holes + probe at first position + empty A-scan. Animatable reveals, timed to narration 'Peak the same size hole at quarter, half, and three-quarter thickness, gain untouched. Connect the marks: a DAC, judging indications at their own depth.' (~9.5s): dacPeakQuarter at ~1.8s = beam line from probe to T/4 hole + tall echo on the A-scan peaking exactly at the 80% line, cyan dot marking the peak tip (narration 'quarter'); dacPeakHalf at ~3.0s = beam to T/2 hole + mid-height echo further along the sweep, dot at tip ('half'); dacPeakThreeQ at ~4.2s = beam to 3T/4 hole + lowest echo, dot ('three-quarter thickness'); dacGainLock at ~5.4s = small padlock/'GAIN LOCKED' badge on the gain readout ('gain untouched'); dacCurve at ~6.4s = smooth falling curve drawn left-to-right through the three peak dots ('connect the marks'); dacJudge at ~8.0s = a smaller sample indication echo appearing under the curve tagged '50% DAC', showing evaluation against the curve at its own depth ('judging indications at their own depth'). The falling curve must visibly encode: same-size reflector, deeper = weaker echo.

mt-two-directions.brief: Why two roughly perpendicular magnetizations are required (600x340 holographic navy/cyan). Flat bar/plate seen face-on with first-direction flux arrows and TWO tight cracks: crack A oriented across the flux, crack B lying parallel to the flux. Reveals: tdFirst (base part + first-direction flux arrows + crack A already decorated with a bright particle indication, 0s, narration 'Interpret what formed'), tdHidden (crack B appears as a ghost outline with NO particles, pulsing faintly, at ~2-4s, narration 'a flaw lying along the flux may not show'), tdRotate (flux arrows sweep roughly 90 degrees to the second direction at ~4.5-6.5s, narration 'magnetize again, roughly perpendicular'), tdCatch (crack B blooms a crisp indication at ~6.5-8.5s, narration 'the second shot catches it', just before the note lands at 7.5s). IMPORTANT: do not label the directions 'circular' or 'longitudinal' (that terminology arrives in mt1-4); if labels are needed use FIRST SHOT / SECOND SHOT.

curie-point.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan graph + part vignette showing ferromagnetism collapsing at the Curie point. LEFT ~65%: axes — horizontal TEMPERATURE scaled 0 to 900 °C, vertical ABILITY TO MAGNETIZE (permeability, no units). A cyan curve starts high and nearly flat, sags gently as temperature rises, then collapses to the baseline at a dashed vertical marker at 770 °C labeled CURIE POINT · 770 °C (IRON); the region right of the marker is lightly hatched and tagged NOT FERROMAGNETIC. RIGHT ~35%: two small part chips stacked — top: orange-glowing hot forging with broken/fizzling flux loop and particles falling away, tag ABOVE — NO GRIP; bottom: cooled navy part with intact flux loops, tag COOLED — TESTABLE. Animatable reveals synced to beat-2 narration (~8.3 s total): curAxes at 0.5 s (axes + labels draw; narration 'Heat works against you'); curCurve at 1.9 s (the gentle declining segment draws left to right; 'as steel heats, permeability falls'); curDrop at 3.9 s (the steep collapse segment to zero; 'and at the Curie point'); curMark at 4.7 s (dashed 770 °C vertical + CURIE POINT label pop; 'about 770 degrees Celsius for iron'); curHot at 6.2 s (hot-part chip + NOT FERROMAGNETIC hatching fade in; 'ferromagnetism vanishes entirely'); curCool at 7.2 s (cooled-part chip fades in, just before the spec note 'ABOVE 770 °C — NO MT UNTIL IT COOLS' lands at 7.4 s).

hysteresis-loops.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan side-by-side comparison of two B-H hysteresis loops, each on its own mini axes (horizontal H — MAGNETIZING FORCE, vertical B — FLUX DENSITY). LEFT: a WIDE, nearly rectangular loop with direction arrows, tag HARD · HEAT-TREATED; bright dot + tick where the loop crosses the B axis labeled HIGH RETENTIVITY (the field the part keeps at zero current). RIGHT: a slim narrow loop hugging the diagonal, tag SOFT · ANNEALED, with a tiny B-axis crossing labeled FIELD GONE AT ONCE. Keep labels minimal — no coercive-force callout (narration does not cover it). Animatable reveals synced to beat-3 narration (~8.6 s total): hysAxes at 0.6 s (both axis pairs draw; narration 'Hardness pulls the other way'); hysWide at 2.0 s (wide loop draws as a closed cycle with arrows; 'draws a wide hysteresis loop'); hysHard at 3.2 s (HARD · HEAT-TREATED tag); hysRet at 4.6 s (retentivity dot + HIGH RETENTIVITY tick glow; 'holds magnetism stubbornly'); hysNarrow at 6.0 s (slim loop draws; 'Soft annealed steel'); hysSoft at 6.9 s (SOFT · ANNEALED tag + FIELD GONE AT ONCE tick; 'lets go almost instantly'). Spec note 'WIDE LOOP HOLDS · NARROW LOOP LETS GO' overlays at 7.6 s.

water-path-echoes.brief: Diagram, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan, split layout. LEFT: vertical section — immersion probe at top, water column below it, steel plate at bottom with thickness dimension labeled T; a dimension bracket spans the water column. RIGHT: A-scan strip (time base with baseline grid). Beat-2 narration (~9.5s): 'Sound is four times faster in steel than water. Set the water path to at least four times thickness. The second surface echo stays clear.' Reveals: wpScene at 0.0s = probe + water column + plate labeled T + empty A-scan grid; wpSpeed at ~0.8s = paired velocity arrows, a short arrow in the water tagged 'WATER ×1' and a long arrow in the steel tagged 'STEEL ×4' ('Sound is four times faster in steel than water'); wpRule at ~3.6s = water-column bracket brightens and gains the label '≥ 4 × T' ('set the water path to at least four times thickness'); wpEchoes at ~6.4s = A-scan pops front-surface echo labeled FS then back-wall echo labeled BW; wpSecond at ~7.6s = second front-surface echo labeled FS2 lands BEYOND BW with the clear gap between BW and FS2 highlighted ('the second surface echo stays clear'). Include a small loop arrow at the entry face hinting FS2 is the front-surface reflection making a second round trip. Overlay note appears at 8.5s (renderer-side), so keep the lower band uncluttered.

curved-surface-beam.brief: Diagram, 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Large round steel bar in cross-section (circle) under a faint waterline, immersion probe above it. Beat-4 narration (~8.7s): 'Curved surfaces act like lenses too. A convex bar spreads the beam. Aim through the center, and match your focus to the radius.' Reveals: csBar at 0.0s = bar cross-section + flat probe above ('Curved surfaces act like lenses too'); csDiverge at ~2.2s = rays from the flat probe refract at the convex entry surface and fan apart inside the bar, dimming with depth ('a convex bar spreads the beam'); csRadial at ~4.6s = dashed aim line from probe through the bar's exact center pulses and the probe axis snaps onto it, plus a small ghost off-axis ray veering sideways with an × marker to motivate center aim ('aim through the center'); csMatched at ~6.6s = probe face swaps to a concave (focused) profile matched to the radius and the refracted rays now converge into a bright concentrated zone inside the bar ('match your focus to the radius'). Overlay note appears at 7.7s (renderer-side); keep bottom strip clear.

plate-wave.brief: Generating a Lamb (plate) wave in immersion (600x340, holographic navy/cyan), side view. A thin horizontal sheet (both surfaces drawn, exaggerated thinness) submerged in water. A small immersion transducer above-left, tilted off vertical, fires a beam that strikes the top surface at a marked incident angle. From the strike point, the energy becomes a guided plate wave drawn as a gentle sinusoidal flexing of the WHOLE sheet thickness traveling right — both surfaces undulate together (the sheet vibrates as a unit; do NOT draw a ray zigzagging inside the sheet). Far right, a small disbond/lamination marker under a bonded doubler patch that the traveling wave reaches and lights up. Sync to my 9s narration: 'In a sheet about a wavelength thick' (0-2.3s), 'a precisely tilted beam' (2.3-3.8s), 'couples into a Lamb wave that rides the sheet' (3.8-6.5s), 'sweeping panels for disbonds' (6.5-9s). Reveals: plwSheet (draw, 0.6s) the thin sheet; plwThickTick (fade, 1.4s) thickness tick paired with a wavelength tick showing thickness is comparable to wavelength; plwProbe (draw, 2.4s) tilted transducer in water; plwBeam (draw, 3.0s) incident beam; plwAngle (fade, 3.6s) incident-angle arc flagged as the precise control; plwGuided (draw, 4.6s) flexing guided wave traveling along the sheet; plwSweep (fade, 6.6s) long reach arrow along the sheet (one setup sweeps a large area); plwDisbond (fade, 7.4s) disbond marker lighting up. Keep bottom-center clear for the player note chip at 8.0s.

echo-vs-through.brief: Split comparison of the two immersion arrangements (600x340, holographic navy/cyan), left vs right halves with a thin divider; both halves show the same rectangular block with a mid-depth flaw. LEFT 'PULSE-ECHO': one downward transducer above the block; beam arrows go down AND return (front surface, flaw, back wall); below the block a mini A-scan strip with three blips on a time axis, with a depth bracket tying the middle blip's time to the flaw's depth. RIGHT 'THROUGH-TRANSMISSION': two transducers facing each other, one above and one below the block, perfectly aligned on one axis (alignment centerline); a ONE-WAY beam arrow from transmitter to receiver; the flaw blocks/scatters the beam, casting a shadow beyond it; below, a single received-signal bar shown dropping; a small struck-through depth bracket cues that depth is unknown — no time axis on this side. Sync to my 9s narration: 'Pulse echo: one probe reads echoes' (0-2.4s), 'echo time gives depth' (2.4-4.0s), 'Through transmission: two aligned probes' (4.0-5.8s), 'A flaw drops the received signal' (5.8-7.6s), 'No depth' (7.6-8.6s). Reveals: evtPeProbe (draw, 0.5s) left transducer + block + flaw; evtPeEchoes (draw, 1.3s) down-and-back arrows off front/flaw/back; evtPeScan (fade, 2.2s) mini A-scan with three blips; evtPeDepth (fade, 3.0s) depth bracket linking blip time to flaw depth; evtTtProbes (draw, 4.4s) aligned probe pair + alignment axis; evtTtBeam (draw, 5.2s) one-way transmitted beam; evtTtFlaw (fade, 5.9s) flaw blocking with shadow; evtTtDrop (fade, 6.6s) received-signal bar dropping; evtTtNoDepth (fade, 7.8s) struck-through depth bracket. Keep bottom-center clear for the player note chip at 7.9s.

beam-arrangements.brief: 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Three side-by-side mini-panels on identical steel-block cross-sections, mirroring lesson figure ut2-1-3-fig-a (pulse-echo / through-transmission / pitch-catch). Reveal ids and timing against the 22-word beat-2 narration (~8.0 s total): (1) baPulseEcho — reveal at 0.3 s with 'Pulse echo reads depth from echo time': left panel, caption PULSE ECHO; one probe on the top face, down-and-back ray to the back wall past a small mid-wall flaw, mini A-scan inset with initial-pulse/flaw/back-wall spikes and arrow tag ECHO TIME → DEPTH. (2) baThruTrans — reveal at 2.9 s with 'Through transmission reads energy loss, no depth': center panel, caption THROUGH-TRANSMISSION; transmitter on top face, aligned receiver on bottom face, one-way ray, mid-wall flaw casting a shadow cone onto the receiver, small received-amplitude bar shown dropping, tag LOSS ONLY. (3) baPitchCatch — reveal at 5.4 s with 'Pitch catch: send and receive split, same side': right panel, caption PITCH-CATCH; two separate elements side by side on the SAME top face, angled transmit ray down, reflected ray rising into the receiving element (V path), tag SAME SIDE. Keep the lower band clear: the renderer overlays the beat note at 7.0 s.

thickness-readout.brief: Title-beat hero panel: a digital thickness gauge measuring a corroded pipe wall. 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Layout: right two-thirds, cutaway cross-section of a pipe wall — smooth outer (top) surface, pitted corroded inner (bottom) surface — with a dual-element probe coupled to the top face over a thin couplant film line. Left third: handheld gauge body (rounded rectangle, two buttons) with a large dot-matrix display and a small 'MM' unit tag. Base art (pipe section, probe, gauge with blank display) is visible from beat start. Beat narration (~7 s): 'A thickness gauge is a small pulse-echo instrument: it times an echo, does the arithmetic, displays one confident number.' Reveals: (1) thrPing at ~3.5 s ('it times an echo') — cyan pulse dot with trailing arc fires from the probe face, travels down through the wall, reflects off the pitted back surface, returns to the probe; loop the round trip about every 1.6 s thereafter, with faint timing ticks along the path. (2) thrCompute at ~5.0 s ('does the arithmetic') — the gauge display digits cycle/flicker rapidly as if computing. (3) thrLock at ~6.2 s ('displays one confident number') — display locks on '25.0' with a confident glow ring and a tiny 'STEEL' material chip lighting under the readout. Keep composition calm; this sits beneath the title card and its 'LIVE READ // 25.0 MM' label.

echo-round-trip.brief: Teaching diagram: how a thickness gauge converts round-trip time into thickness, T = v x t / 2, including the lesson's worked example. 600x340 holographic navy/cyan. Layout: left half, vertical cross-section slab of steel wall with a probe coupled on the top face and the back wall at the bottom; depth bracket along the slab's left edge labeled 'T = ?'; small material tag 'STEEL · 5.92 mm/µs' beside the slab. Right half: a stopwatch dial with a digital microsecond readout beneath it, and a formula card below that. Beat narration (~9.5 s): 'Thickness is velocity times time over two — the two because sound travels down and back. In steel, eight point four five microseconds reads twenty-five millimeters.' Reveals in narration order: (1) ertFormula at ~1.0 s ('velocity times time over two') — formula card types on 'T = v x t ÷ 2' with the '÷ 2' rendered dim for now. (2) ertPath at ~3.6 s ('sound travels down and back') — pulse dot leaves the probe, travels down, bounces off the back wall, returns; the two legs leave dashed trails labeled 'DOWN' and 'BACK'; the stopwatch hand sweeps while its readout counts up and freezes at '8.45 µs' when the echo arrives; the dim '÷ 2' brightens with a pulse ring (one wall, crossed twice). (3) ertSolve at ~6.0 s ('In steel ... twenty-five millimeters') — worked line slides in under the formula: '5.92 x 8.45 ÷ 2 = 25.0 mm', and the depth bracket label flips from 'T = ?' to '25.0 mm' with a glow. The spec's note line 'DOWN AND BACK — SO DIVIDE BY TWO' lands in the lower third at ~8.8 s, so keep that band clear.

shaft-two-direction-plan.brief: Two-direction bench plan for a hardened pump shaft (lesson fig-a content), 600x340 navy/cyan. A long horizontal shaft with machined journals and fillets clamped between two headstock contact pads of a wet bench; a 5-turn coil sits ghosted around mid-shaft. Reveals: stpBench = shaft + headstock contacts, on from 0s. stpCircular = current arrows entering through the head contacts plus circular field hoops wrapping around the shaft, at ~1.5s when narration says 'the head shot's circular field'. stpAxialSeam = a thin seam line running ALONG the shaft axis glowing with a particle indication at ~3s on 'catches axial seams'. stpCoil = the coil solidifying with longitudinal flux lines running down the axis at ~5s on 'the coil's longitudinal field'. stpTransCrack = a transverse crack across a journal fillet glowing with an indication at ~7s on 'transverse cracks at journals and fillets'. Keep both field systems visually distinct (hoops vs axial lines); the renderer's caption note appears at 8.4s, beat runs ~10s.

offset-central-conductor.brief: Offset central conductor on a ring gear (lesson fig-b content), 600x340 navy/cyan. Three-quarter view of a toothed ring gear with a large bore resting ON an insulated copper bar that passes through the bore, bar against the bore wall (offset, not centered). Reveals: occGear = gear outline + bore, on from 0s. occBar = the insulated bar through the bore with current arrows INSIDE THE BAR ONLY (never in the gear) plus a small NO CONTACT tag, at ~1s on 'no electrical contact'. occField = concentric circular field rings around the bar, strongest where bar touches the bore wall and fading with distance, at ~3s on 'an offset central conductor'. occBand = a bright coverage band on the bore arc nearest the bar spanning about four conductor diameters, with a '4 x conductor dia' dimension tick, at ~4.5s on 'four conductor diameters per shot'. occRotate = a rotation arrow on the gear plus two or three ghosted successive coverage bands overlapping ~10% as they step around the circumference, at ~7s on 'rotate through overlapping shots'. Renderer note appears at 8.2s, beat runs ~9.5s.
